mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and signals easier financial conditions, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-down, or if the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal activates when unemployment rises faster than payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns at a higher rate than untriggered periods, or if SPX is positive in most cases despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST reflects broad domestic demand strength, which should lift cyclicals and the index over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if retail sales improve while housing remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upswings in RSAFS and HOUST do not coincide with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and future earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is clearest when GDP is also decelerating.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-average INDPRO readings do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the sign remains positive during clear industrial contractions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST after a prior slump indicates an emerging housing rebound that improves wealth effects and construction activity, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebound triggers are followed by flat or negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect disappears outside rebound periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, policy restraint and inflation pressure combine to compress valuation multiples and hurt equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, high-inflation episodes fail to generate negative 20-day SPX returns, or if FEDFUNDS alone explains the outcome without CPIAUCSL confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: A declining PAYEMS trend indicates weakening labor demand and lower household spending power, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when UNRATE is also rising.
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll declines and unemployment increases do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is not stronger than either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in GDPC1 identifies a growth uptrend that should support corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should fade when CPIAUCSL is accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if rising GDP periods do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the effect persists equally during high-inflation periods.
